page: Microsoft Azure D32d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX-512 vector extensions, AES cryptography, and Intel Hyper-Threading. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 1229 GB of local storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the D32d_v4 in the average tier across most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1413.36, while the multi-core score reaches 29808.15. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1661.99 GB/sec, which drops to 105.49 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and a write speed of 46.46 GB/sec. In database operations, the server achieves a PassMark database score of 10521.57 and handles 26.79 million Redis SET operations per second.

Tradeoffs are evident in specific workloads. Single-threaded gzip compression is a weak point, registering at 23.49 GB/sec, and large-scale LLM inference is highly constrained, with 70B model prompt processing performing poorly at 4.65 tokens/sec. However, the server demonstrates solid capabilities for multi-threaded tasks, such as bzip3 decompression at 130.92 GB/sec and software compilation with Clang scoring 22504.0. Given the balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and substantial local storage, this server is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, relational databases, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor. It features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including a multi-core stress-ng score of 29808.15 and a Redis SET throughput of 26.79 million operations per second. While single-threaded gzip compression and large LLM inference represent performance bottlenecks, the system excels in multi-threaded tasks like Clang software compilation and bzip3 decompression. The balanced resource allocation and high-capacity local storage make this instance suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering balanced multi-core CPU and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32d_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high-capacity local storage, and reliable multi-core performance for enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM
1229 GB of bundled local storage for data-intensive tasks
Average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 29808.15
Handles 26.79 million Redis SET operations per second
Underperforms on single-threaded compression and large LLM inference
Appropriate for database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation
